CREATE TABLE test_dates (id UInt32, date_value Date, datetime_value DateTime, datetime64_value DateTime64(3));
INSERT INTO test_dates VALUES (1, toDate('1999-01-01'), toDateTime('1999-01-01 00:00:00'), toDateTime64('1999-01-01 00:00:00.000', 3));
SELECT changeSecond(date_value, 15) FROM test_dates WHERE id = 1;
SELECT changeSecond(datetime_value, 0) FROM test_dates WHERE id = 1;
SELECT changeSecond(datetime64_value, 45) FROM test_dates WHERE id = 1;
SELECT changeSecond(toDate('2000-02-29'), 30) AS new_date, changeSecond(toDateTime('2000-02-29 23:59:59'), 59) AS new_datetime;
SELECT id, changeSecond(datetime_value, 15) FROM (SELECT id, datetime_value FROM test_dates WHERE id = 1) AS subquery;
SELECT changeSecond(x, 10) FROM (SELECT date_value AS x FROM test_dates WHERE id = 1) AS inner_query;
CREATE TABLE test_dates_2 (id UInt32, date_value Date, datetime_value DateTime, datetime64_value DateTime64(3));
INSERT INTO test_dates_2 VALUES (1, toDate('1998-12-31'), toDateTime('1998-12-31 23:59:45'), toDateTime64('1998-12-31 23:59:40.123', 3));
SELECT changeSecond((SELECT datetime_value FROM test_dates WHERE id = 1), 20) AS modified_datetime;
SELECT id FROM test_dates_2 WHERE changeSecond(datetime_value, 10) > toDateTime('1998-12-31 23:59:00');
SELECT changeSecond(datetime64_value, 59) AS last_second FROM (SELECT datetime64_value FROM test_dates WHERE id = 1) AS temp;
SELECT d.id, changeSecond(d.datetime_value, 25) AS updated_time FROM test_dates d JOIN test_dates_2 dd ON d.id = dd.id;
SELECT changeSecond(toDateTime64('2010-01-01 00:00:00.000', 3), CAST(50 AS UInt8)) AS modified_datetime64, changeSecond(toDateTime('2010-01-01 00:00:00'), CAST(50 AS UInt8)) AS modified_datetime;
